---
title: "Comparison: seqtte Stata package"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
jupyter: nbstata
---

This document runs sequential target trial emulation using the `seqtte` Stata
package on the `trial_example` dataset. It is the companion to
`comparison-trialemulation.qmd`, which runs the same analysis using the
TrialEmulation R package.

**Prerequisite.** Render `comparison-trialemulation.qmd` first; that document
saves `trial_example.dta` into the `cscripts/` directory used here.

## Setup

Add the package root to the ado-path so `seqtte` can be found when rendering
from this directory.

```{stata}
adopath ++ ".."
which seqtte
```

## Data

```{stata}
use "trial_example.dta", clear
describe
```

```{stata}
summarize period outcome treatment catvarA catvarB nvarA nvarB nvarC
```

**Note on eligibility.** `seqtte` determines eligibility internally as
"not previously treated" (A\_lag ≠ 1). The `eligible` column in
`trial_example` reflects additional eligibility criteria used by TrialEmulation,
so small differences in estimates between the two documents are expected.

## Intent-to-treat (ITT) analysis

The model adjusts for `catvarA`, `catvarB`, `nvarA`, `nvarB`, and `nvarC`
(matching the TrialEmulation covariates). Quadratic polynomial terms for
follow-up time and trial (calendar) period are included automatically by
`seqtte`. Standard errors are clustered by individual.

```{stata}
seqtte outcome, id(id) time(period) treatment(treatment) ///
    covariates(catvarA catvarB nvarA nvarB nvarC)
```

## Comparison with TrialEmulation

The TrialEmulation R package produces the following ITT estimates on the same
dataset and covariate specification:

| Package        | log-OR  | SE     | OR    | 95% CI            |
|----------------|---------|--------|-------|-------------------|
| TrialEmulation | −0.2829 | 0.0462 | 0.754 | (0.688, 0.825)    |
| seqtte         | (above) | —      | —     | —                 |

Differences are expected to be small and attributable to the differing
eligibility criteria (see note above).
